behavior: Ground-foraging thrush that hops and scrapes through leaf litter; often flicks its wings and slowly bobs or raises its tail.
field_marks: Warm reddish-brown tail contrasting with the browner back
Thin pale eye-ring around a dark eye
Pale underparts with dark spots on the throat and smudgy breast spotting
Round-headed, full-bodied profile with a slender straight bill and fairly long tail
habitat: Forest understory and floor, especially in deciduous, coniferous, or mixed woodland with edges, openings, shrubs, and leaf litter.

sources.title: Hermit Thrush Identification — All About Birds
